Kyung Hee University · Chemical Engineering

Decoding Electrochemical Interfaces, Degradation, and Corrosion

Yoon Lab develops quantitative electrochemical diagnostics to reveal hidden interfacial reactions, transport limitations, side reactions, and failure mechanisms in energy materials and devices.

Research → Publications → Join Us →
About Yoon Lab

Our research bridges electrochemical analysis, materials characterization, and interface engineering to advance batteries, solid-state electrolytes, corrosion science, supercapacitors, seawater electrolysis, and electrochemical sensors. We aim to establish mechanistic links between measured electrochemical signals and real interfacial phenomena.

Research Themes

Signal-based understanding of electrochemical systems

We combine electrochemical measurements, transient analysis, materials characterization, and modeling to decode complex interfacial and degradation processes.

Electrochemical Interfaces and Degradation

We investigate interfacial side reactions, SEI/CEI formation, transport limitations, and degradation pathways in rechargeable batteries and electrochemical devices.

Corrosion in Energy Materials

We quantify corrosion, passivation failure, and current-collector degradation in aqueous, non-aqueous, and solid-state battery environments.

Solid-State and Polymer Electrolytes

We study sulfide-based solid electrolytes, polymer electrolytes, and solid-solid interfaces for safer and more durable lithium batteries.

Advanced Electrochemical Diagnostics

We develop signal-based diagnostics using EIS/DRT, PITT/GITT, QCM, RRDE, transient analysis, and electrochemical modeling.

Featured Research Highlights

Recent achievements

2026
Seawater electrolysis

Applied Catalysis B: Environment and Energy

Facet-resolved mechanistic insights into OER-selective seawater electrolysis on Co₃O₄.

2025
Solid-state batteries

Advanced Functional Materials

Impact of electrolyte decomposition on copper corrosion in Li₆PS₅Cl-based all-solid-state batteries.

2025
Supercapacitors

Chemical Engineering Journal

Aqueous supercapacitors with ultrawide potential window and pH-independent stability.

2023
Silicon anodes

Advanced Energy Materials

Inhibition of silicon fracture via rigid solid electrolyte interphase in lithium-ion batteries.

News & Opportunities

Updates

New member

Uihyeon Lee joined Yoon Lab through the early-start graduate admission track

Uihyeon Lee started graduate research in Yoon Lab through the early-start graduate admission track before the regular Fall 2026 graduate semester. Welcome!

2026-09-01
Recruiting

Recruiting graduate students for Spring 2027 admission

Yoon Lab is recruiting motivated MS, PhD, and combined MS–PhD students for Spring 2027 admission. Students interested in electrochemical diagnostics, degradation, corrosion, solid-state and polymer electrolytes, or battery safety are encouraged to contact us.

2026-07-07
Publication

Paper accepted in Applied Catalysis B: Environment and Energy

Our work on facet-resolved mechanistic insights into OER-selective seawater electrolysis on Co₃O₄ has been accepted. Congratulations to the authors!

2026-01-15

Join Yoon Lab

We welcome motivated students interested in batteries, electrochemical diagnostics, corrosion, solid-state electrolytes, polymer electrolytes, and quantitative analysis of interfacial reactions.
